events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need assess field condition: damper positions vs. balance report mismatch, HVAC-R terminations & balancing, rough-complete. Need decide pass/fail/needs more, risk, etc. Condition is out of tolerance because actual damper positions don't match TAB designated settings. That impacts airflow balance, comfort, energy, possibly system performance. It's non-compliant and installed-defective. Decision fail (non-compliant). Risk medium? Mismatch may cause poor airflow, but not immediate safety hazard. Could be medium. Work stage rough-complete. Percent complete maybe 70-85? Component conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. Value? Need estimate numeric reading? The task says estimate or interpret measurable condition. We can set value maybe 0 representing mismatch? Or represent deviation? The schema says value numeric reading or computed quantity when task asks. Here task says estimate or interpret measurable condition and state why out of tolerance. We could set value to mismatch count/percentage? Since no specific numbers, value maybe 0 (no numeric reading). But better set value to 100% mismatch? Hmm. Confidence high? Observed mismatch. Event_time 0, rate 0, sound_source empty. Order and workable empty arrays. Findings: actual damper positions do not match final TAB report, balancing not verified. Actions: adjust dampers to match TAB report, re-verify airflow and document. Rationale: SMACNA requires dampers be set and locked per approved balance report; deviation beyond specified tolerance is non-compliant and defeats system balance. References: SMACNA HVAC Systems Testing, Adjusting and Balancing; project TAB report. Need ensure no markdown. JSON raw. Need include all fields.

events.label: response
events.text: {"decision":"fail","risk":"medium","work_stage":"rough-complete","component_conditions":["installed-defective","non-compliant"],"percent_complete":80,"value":0,"sound_source":"","confidence":0.85,"event_time":0.0,"rate":0.0,"order":[],"workable":[],"findings":["Observed damper positions do not agree with the final TAB balance report.","System airflow balance has not been verified or maintained as required."],"actions":["Adjust all dampers to the positions specified in the approved TAB report.","Lock or tag dampers after setting and re-measure airflow to confirm balance.","Update field documentation and reconcile discrepancies with the TAB contractor."],"rationale":"SMACNA and standard TAB practice require installed dampers to be set, locked, and left in the position recorded on the approved balance report. A visible mismatch means the system is not balanced within tolerance, so the work is non-compliant and defective until corrected and reverified.","references":["SMACNA HVAC Systems Testing, Adjusting and Balancing","Project TAB report / approved balance settings"]}
